summary: A rectangular, single-story stylized building with a stepped central roof and four tall corner towers topped by flat overhanging caps. Exterior walls are layered horizontally with light and darker bands; white vertical corner/wall accents and a row of white blocks form a low perimeter detail. The structure sits on a cleared dirt pad with a recessed inner courtyard area.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main hall with four corner towers and a recessed central roof section
shape.symmetry: approximately bilateral symmetry along the long axis

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: white vertical accents and perimeter low blocks

materials.name: wood
materials.role: possible inner horizontal band (darker stripe) or interior floors

elements: four tall corner towers with overhanging flat caps
horizontal banded walls with alternating light/dark stripes
white vertical buttresses at wall intervals
row of white blocks forming a low perimeter sill
small square windows on tower faces
ground cleared dirt pad and recessed inner area
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular footprint with long axis matching the stepped roof orientation; include an inward recessed central roof section.

Place four towers at the corners; towers are taller than main roof and have stonebrick-like corner trim with small windows and a flat overhanging cap of red tile surrounded by light slab edging and a central glow block.

Construct main walls as horizontal bands: alternating light sandstone-like bands and darker thin stripes; add white vertical 1-2 block-wide pillars at intervals and a perimeter row of white blocks set one block out from wall base.
Create stepped roof using stacked slab/step layers (light gray slabs for edges, red bricks for top tiles) decreasing toward center ridge; a small raised rectangular skylight or accent on top center.
Use small glass panes for tower windows arranged vertically; add subtle recessed courtyard or lowered ground inside the white perimeter.
uncertainties: Exact block types for light/dark horizontal wall bands: appears to be sandstone and a darker wood or terracotta but could be another material.
Roof edge material: looks like stone slabs (light) with brick top; the slab type (stone vs. quartz slab) is uncertain.
Precise internal layout and floor materials are not visible.
Exact footprint dimensions are estimated from images; spacing between towers and wall thicknesses may differ.
